rationale: The citing paper uses Labroo and Lee (2006) in its theory-building section to support a specific premise: processing fluency can affect purchase intentions. That premise is then carried into the paper's own mechanism, where frame-construal match is argued to increase fluency and ultimately improve recycling intentions and behavior. Although the citation is brief, it plays a meaningful conceptual support role rather than a purely incidental mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Labroo and Lee (2006) as prior evidence that fluency has downstream effects on intentions. It uses that result as part of the conceptual foundation for its proposed fluency-based pathway to recycling outcomes.
